Description: Use a consistent spacing scale (e.g., 4px, 8px, 12px, 16px, 24px, 32px, 48px, 64px). All spacing should come from this scale.
Create visual rhythm through varied spacing—tight groupings, generous separations. Not the same padding everywhere.
Use CSS Grid or Flexbox for layout. Grid provides structure and alignment.
Don't: Use floats or absolute positioning for layout

Description: Break the grid intentionally for emphasis. Not everything needs to align perfectly.
Use container queries (@container) for component-level responsiveness instead of only viewport-based breakpoints.
Use clamp() for fluid spacing that breathes on larger screens.
Use whitespace intentionally, not just leftover. Whitespace creates breathing room and hierarchy.
Use size to communicate importance. Larger elements draw attention first.
Related items should be closer together (proximity principle). Unrelated items should have more space.
